content: I may be missing something obvious, but do locks actually need to be immovable when unlocked? When the "Runtime locking correctness validator" I don't think it is necessary. When it is enabled I can't find why it is necessary either. When they are locked, it is understandable that they need to be immovable, but the lock guard should already ensure this as it immutably borrows the lock.
I'm pretty sure at least some lock types are self-referential when in debug mode.
You can forget the lock guard and move the locked lock though.
